proof: ingest diff-apply live receipt — search latency during natural daemon ticks, idle CPU, matrix overwrite rate
context: replaceSession (DELETE-all+reinsert-all, single sync tx) replaced by row-level diff apply in 64-row chunk txs with event-loop yields; embed jobs delta-gated; fingerprint stamped last under an applying: sentinel. Pre-fix live behavior: 36.5s ingest bursts head-of-line blocking searches to 24.4s server-side, /ready outliers 4.7-12.3s, idle CPU 96-157%, ~50 matrix overwrites/min, watermark matrix>sqlite ghost lead (+127).
method: 360 probes over 180s (2/s, thirds lexical/semantic/fusion, half warm fixed query / half novel uuid queries) against http://127.0.0.1:7180 while the 60s ingest daemon re-applied active sessions (10 session POSTs observed in a 4-min window incl. the multi-thousand-row live session driving this work). Client wall via perf_counter around urllib; server searchMs/embedMs from response receipts.
ingestSpansPostFix: session applies logged at 27-127ms typical; one 7556ms large-delta apply (span covers the whole chunked request; loop yields between chunks, and no concurrent search exceeded 288.5ms — span duration no longer implies blocking)

matrixChurn.watermark: matrix 694,256 vs sqlite 694,282 — matrix trails by 26 (healthy append lag; pre-fix it LED by ~127 ghost slots from vector AD-trigger wipes)

novelClientP95.note: embed budget ~60-80ms rides on top of scan; consistent with the pre-existing novel budget line, unrelated to this change

daemonIntervalDecision: left at 60s — the interval bump was a mitigation for server-side churn that no longer exists (idle 0.81-2.88% WITH the daemon ticking); remaining client-side re-map cost of the stale 0.2.3 binary is a distribution issue, resolved by the 0.3.0 publish, not by pacing.
